criterion_name: Excel Vital Info in Cell A1
visual_checks: CRITICAL — CELL A1 INSPECTION: Navigate to cell A1 of the first sheet in the workbook. Does it contain content that immediately tells the user what this spreadsheet is about or where to find instructions?
Check whether A1 contains a meaningful document title, description, or purpose statement (e.g., 'FY2025 Budget Summary' or 'Employee Directory — Updated March 2025').
If A1 contains a link, verify it points to instructions, a table of contents, or an overview sheet that helps orient the user.
Check whether A1 is a merged cell spanning multiple columns — while the content may be present, merged cells at A1 can cause navigation issues.
If A1 is empty, check whether the content starts at some other cell (B1, A2, A5, etc.) and the user would have to search for it.
Verify that cell A1 is not part of a decorative merged header that contains only a logo or non-descriptive text.
Check the same for additional sheets: does A1 on each sheet identify the sheet's purpose, or is it empty/irrelevant?
pass_conditions: Cell A1 on the first sheet contains a meaningful title or description that identifies the document's purpose
If the workbook requires instructions, A1 contains a link to the instructions sheet or an overview of the workbook
Screen readers landing on A1 immediately hear useful context about the spreadsheet's content
A1 is not part of an excessively merged region that complicates navigation
Each sheet's A1 cell contains identifying information for that sheet's content
fail_conditions: Cell A1 on the first sheet is empty — a screen reader user landing in the default position hears nothing and must search for content
Cell A1 contains irrelevant data (e.g., a cell is part of a decorative border, contains only a space character, or holds a non-descriptive value like '1' or 'x')
Cell A1 is a merged cell containing only a logo image with no text or alt text
The actual identifying content of the spreadsheet starts at a distant cell (e.g., A5, B3, D1) with no indication in A1 of where to navigate
Cell A1 contains outdated or incorrect information about the document
na_conditions: Document is not a spreadsheet (not XLSX, XLS, ODS, or similar)
Spreadsheet is designed as a data export or machine-readable file not intended for direct human consumption
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Spreadsheet could not be opened or cell A1 could not be inspected
File format does not present cells in a grid layout
common_mistakes: Leaving A1 empty because the data table starts at row 2 or lower (row 1 is left blank for visual spacing)
Merging A1 across many columns for a title banner, which makes the merged cell difficult to navigate and can confuse screen readers about the column structure
Placing a logo image in A1 without any alt text or accompanying text — screen readers may announce nothing or just 'image'
Starting meaningful content at B1 or A2 because A1 has a small formatting element or checkbox
Having A1 contain residual content from a template (e.g., 'Enter title here' or '[Title]') that was never updated
Putting critical navigation instructions in a cell far from A1 that screen reader users would never find naturally

examples.pass: Cell A1 on the first sheet reads 'FY2025 Departmental Budget — City of Richlands'. A screen reader user opening the file immediately hears the document's identity and purpose. Each subsequent sheet has A1 containing the sheet's topic (e.g., 'Public Works Budget', 'Parks and Recreation Budget').
examples.fail: Cell A1 on the first sheet is empty. The actual title 'Annual Report Data' is in a merged cell at C3. A screen reader user opening the file hears silence at A1 and has no indication of where to navigate or what the spreadsheet contains.
examples.partial: The first sheet's A1 contains a descriptive title, but three other sheets in the workbook have empty A1 cells with content starting at various positions.
